item.title: Singlet oxygen explained
item.description: In this video I show you how the light for one of the world's most poserful laser's is made. The COIL laser for the YAL-1 laser uses light that is easily made by combining two chemicals to produce singlet oxygen

media:content.media:title: Singlet oxygen explained
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you how the light for one of the world's most poserful laser's is made. The COIL laser for the YAL-1 laser uses light that is easily made by combining two chemicals to produce singlet oxygen

item.title: How can glass be cut underwater?
item.description: A science experiment reveals an unusual way glass can be cut underwater, with a result that’s stranger than it sounds.

media:content.media:title: How can glass be cut underwater?
media:content.media:description: A science experiment reveals an unusual way glass can be cut underwater, with a result that’s stranger than it sounds.

item.title: Soap film holds in a surprising way
item.description: A soap film appears to hold in place, revealing a simple physics effect that makes the unexpected feel obvious.

media:content.media:title: Soap film holds in a surprising way
media:content.media:description: A soap film appears to hold in place, revealing a simple physics effect that makes the unexpected feel obvious.

item.title: A robot and Asimov's first law
item.description: I built a robot that breaks the first law of robotics and then show you Dax who is the opposite of that.

media:content.media:title: A robot and Asimov's first law
media:content.media:description: I built a robot that breaks the first law of robotics and then show you Dax who is the opposite of that.

item.title: Don't shake this liquid too hard
item.description: I show you the chemical stoplight

media:content.media:title: Don't shake this liquid too hard
media:content.media:description: I show you the chemical stoplight

item.title: A spider can do that?
item.description: A spider can do that? The explanation is weirder than it sounds

media:content.media:title: A spider can do that?
media:content.media:description: A spider can do that? The explanation is weirder than it sounds

item.title: The coldest ball
item.description: In this video I show you what happens when you cool a giant 2" steel ball down to -321°F in liquid nitrogen and then put it in water! It instantly forms clear ice around the ball and starts to bubble. I explain why this happens and also I explain that ice formation is actually exothermic.

media:content.media:title: The coldest ball
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you what happens when you cool a giant 2" steel ball down to -321°F in liquid nitrogen and then put it in water! It instantly forms clear ice around the ball and starts to bubble. I explain why this happens and also I explain that ice formation is actually exothermic.

item.title: This ice cream trick feels impossible
item.description: I show you how ice cream can transfer both ways when two flavors touch. I explain the difference between mixing and stirring and how it relates to entropy.

media:content.media:title: This ice cream trick feels impossible
media:content.media:description: I show you how ice cream can transfer both ways when two flavors touch. I explain the difference between mixing and stirring and how it relates to entropy.

item.title: A floating fire ball explained
item.description: I make floating fire with a lighter

media:content.media:title: A floating fire ball explained
media:content.media:description: I make floating fire with a lighter

item.title: The world's hottest candle
item.description: In this video I show you a plasma candle made from an ultra high frequency solid state tesla coil (HFSSTC). I talk about why it was tripping the breakers in my house and show you how hot these plasma candles actually are. I talk about why the color of the "flame" is white vs blue or violet.

media:content.media:title: The world's hottest candle
media:content.media:description: In this video I show you a plasma candle made from an ultra high frequency solid state tesla coil (HFSSTC). I talk about why it was tripping the breakers in my house and show you how hot these plasma candles actually are. I talk about why the color of the "flame" is white vs blue or violet.
